message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker and br_history snapshots

Add 39 new issue entries and 212 br_history snapshot files tracking
beads viewer rust development progress.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,6 +27,45 @@
 {"id":"bd-23l","title":"Close TUI graph/insights interaction parity","description":"Close remaining TUI keyboard/visual parity gaps in graph and insights modes. Richer graph interaction (node selection, edge highlighting, expand/collapse) and insights panel navigation need full legacy parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:03:01.989133250Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:40:04.933940726Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:40:04.933922202Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-25a","title":"Fix 5 CI conformance test failures (insights/priority/burndown)","description":"CI conformance tests fail: 2 insights tests (wrong key path/casing), 2 priority tests (count mismatch + missing impact_score), 1 burndown test (off-by-one in daily_points). Also e2e_robot_matrix.rs not committed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:44:33.941802289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T06:07:28.791338570Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T06:07:28.791311860Z","close_reason":"All 5 conformance tests pass. Burndown off-by-one fixed, insights key casing fixed, priority field mapping fixed. E2E and stress test files ready to commit.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-28c","title":"Close TUI board detail shortcut parity gaps","description":"Close remaining TUI keyboard/visual parity gaps in board mode. Board detail panel shortcuts need deeper implementation to match legacy bv behavior - including issue action shortcuts from the detail view and richer board-mode interaction.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:02:58.310513616Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:29:19.765646279Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T04:29:19.765628075Z","close_reason":"Implemented board-detail shortcut parity (focus-preserving lane/row/search navigation) with targeted unit coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl","title":"Legacy capability parity recovery program: replace stale completion claims with verified coverage","description":"Background:
The currently closed parity umbrellas and docs claim a level of completion that we cannot responsibly assert from the present evidence. The codebase is strong and broad, but the project still needs a disciplined capability-parity program that distinguishes between implemented behavior, tested behavior, and merely documented intent.

Goal:
Create the canonical issue tree for getting `bvr` to a truthful state where it can do everything legacy `bv` can do for real operator and agent workflows, while allowing Rust-only improvements and better TUI presentation. Pixel matching is explicitly not the goal; workflow, behavior, and operator outcomes are.

Scope:
This epic covers parity auditing, implementation of newly discovered gaps, proof harness expansion, and documentation truthfulness. Each child should be self-contained enough that a future agent can work from the bead alone without reopening old planning markdown.

Acceptance:
1. The parity claim is backed by evidence instead of optimism.
2. Core legacy workflows are either implemented, proven equivalent, or explicitly de-scoped with rationale.
3. The final tracker tree itself is sufficient as the project plan.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.516406660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:51.981891787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["legacy-port","meta","parity"],"comments":[{"id":128,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Program note:
This tree intentionally supersedes the stale "full parity achieved" narrative. Use it to answer a narrower but more honest question: can `bvr` do every job legacy `bv` can do for robot users and terminal operators? If yes, prove it. If not, create or link a concrete fix bead. Visual improvement is allowed; unsupported workflow regressions are not.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:42Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.1","title":"Build the parity baseline and gap ledger from code and real workflows","description":"Background:
Before changing more code, we need a fresh baseline that compares what legacy `bv` actually does against what `bvr` demonstrably does today. The previous parity closure mixed real evidence with forward-looking assertions.

Goal:
Turn the tracker into the new source of truth for parity status. This track should inventory legacy workflows, define evidence standards, and record a concrete gap ledger that downstream implementation tracks can execute against.

Acceptance:
- The tracker contains a capability matrix spanning robot CLI, loader/model semantics, TUI workflows, and export/pages integrations.
- Every gap has a child bead or an explicit de-scope rationale.
- The final matrix distinguishes implemented, proven, partial, divergent-by-design, and unknown states.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.632806927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.042270553Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.632806927Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","title":"Inventory legacy capability surfaces and map them to concrete bvr modules and workflows","description":"Purpose:
Create the canonical capability inventory that downstream beads rely on. This should enumerate, in tracker form, the legacy surfaces that matter: loader semantics, robot outputs, TUI operator workflows, export/pages flows, workspace aggregation, hooks, preview/watch loops, and path/config resolution behavior.

What to capture:
- The user-visible workflow or operator goal.
- The legacy implementation anchor (docs, code area, or fixture).
- The corresponding Rust modules and tests.
- Whether current status appears proven, partial, unknown, or divergent.

Done means:
Future agents can understand the full scope of parity work from the tracker alone.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.590899400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:42.266195218Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.590899400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","title":"Define the evidence standard for parity claims so beads can be closed honestly","description":"Purpose:
Define what counts as sufficient evidence for saying a surface is done. Some parity claims can be established by fixture-backed conformance; others need TUI journey coverage, shell e2e, or targeted regression tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Different evidence classes are spelled out for robot, TUI, export/pages, and workspace/hook surfaces.
- The plan distinguishes proof of implementation from proof of behavioral equivalence.
- Closing guidance is strict enough that we do not recreate the current overclaim problem.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","title":"Record the fresh gap ledger with explicit statuses: proven, partial, unknown, divergent-by-design","description":"Purpose:
Translate the inventory and evidence standard into an actionable gap ledger. This is the gate between planning and implementation.

Requirements:
- Every material gap becomes a child bead in one of the execution tracks.
- Every currently claimed-complete surface is either revalidated or downgraded.
- Any intentional divergence must include why it is acceptable and what user outcome remains covered.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:22.960647638Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","gaps","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:21.706308818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:22.959815646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2","title":"Audit and close robot/CLI parity gaps against legacy bv semantics","description":"Background:
Robot-mode parity is the most objective and automation-critical surface. Even small path-resolution, filtering, metadata-shape, or loader-semantic mismatches can break downstream agent workflows.

Goal:
Systematically re-audit all legacy robot and CLI behaviors against the Rust port, then fix any remaining gaps in semantics, outputs, or workflow compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Loader, model, and path-resolution semantics are rechecked against legacy behavior.
- Every legacy robot command family has explicit parity evidence or a documented intentional divergence.
- Integration and conformance coverage exists for all reopened gaps.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.077749232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","title":"Re-audit loader, model, and path-resolution semantics against legacy behavior","description":"Purpose:
Audit the subtle compatibility layer that everything else depends on: `.beads` discovery, workspace/repo-path selection, warning suppression in robot mode, JSONL file precedence, BOM handling, malformed-line behavior, model validation, and status/dependency semantics.

Why this matters:
Many operator-visible parity problems are really path or loader mismatches in disguise. This bead should flush out those root causes before they continue leaking into downstream surfaces.

Done means:
There is a concrete list of matching semantics, mismatches, and required fixes/tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.265734858Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.264893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","title":"Re-audit each robot command family for behavioral and output-shape equivalence","description":"Purpose:
Review every legacy robot family again with fresh eyes: triage, next, plan, insights, priority, diff, history, forecast, capacity, burndown, suggest, graph, search, label intelligence, recipes/scripts, docs/schema, and admin or operational flags.

Focus:
- Field shape and omission rules.
- Filter semantics and default behavior.
- Path/config resolution.
- Error/warning behavior and deterministic ordering.

Done means:
Every reopened mismatch becomes either a fix bead or a justified divergence note.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:25.154894155Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:25.154059909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","title":"Close reopened robot/CLI mismatches discovered by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
This is the implementation bucket for concrete robot/CLI bugs exposed by the fresh audit. Do not close it based on planning alone. Populate it via child comments or follow-up beads that reference the exact mismatch and root cause.

Acceptance:
- Every fix includes targeted regression coverage.
- New path/config behaviors are covered from the CLI entrypoint, not only internal helpers.
- The final state preserves determinism and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly documented.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.741578308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.047404675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.598234256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":131,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
This is now a coordination container. Actual implementation should usually land on `bd-2bpl.2.3.1` and/or `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, with each code change tied back to a specific audited mismatch.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","title":"Fix loader/model/path-resolution mismatches surfaced by the robot audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes for loader, model, and path-resolution mismatches discovered in `bd-2bpl.2.1`. Keep this bead focused on discovery/root-cause classes that affect all downstream robot workflows, not on per-command payload details.

Required scope:
- `.beads` and workspace discovery semantics.
- `--repo-path`, `--workspace`, and `--beads-file` precedence and project-root inference.
- JSONL precedence, BOM/malformed-line handling, warning suppression, and validation semantics.
- Model/status/dependency interpretation differences that can change graph shape or command output.

Acceptance:
- Root-cause fixes are implemented without reducing existing functionality.
- Regressions are covered with targeted unit and CLI-entry integration tests.
- Any intentional divergence is recorded explicitly in comments or follow-up beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.140207150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.139341113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","title":"Fix robot command/output/filter/default mismatches surfaced by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete robot-mode fixes from `bd-2bpl.2.2`: payload shape, omission rules, sorting/determinism, filter semantics, default behaviors, and operational/admin command compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Every landed change references a specific audited mismatch and root cause.
- Fixes preserve deterministic output and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly approved and documented.
- Existing features remain available; no simplification that drops capability is allowed.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.359861939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.674097358Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.673284591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.359861939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","title":"Expand conformance and integration harnesses for any reopened robot gaps","description":"Purpose:
Once gaps are identified, the harness must prove them closed. This bead covers adding or tightening fixture-backed conformance, schema validation, and integration tests so the same categories of mismatch do not regress.

Done means:
The evidence burden for robot parity is encoded in tests, not tribal memory.","design":"Testing ladder:
1. Module or helper-level unit tests for root-cause semantics.
2. Conformance fixtures or schema checks where legacy output shape is the source of truth.
3. Binary/integration coverage from the real CLI entrypoint.
4. When end-to-end operator workflow matters, shell/e2e scripts with detailed stdout/stderr and replay logging.

Do not close this bead on narrow unit coverage alone.","acceptance_criteria":"- Reopened robot gaps are covered by the full appropriate testing ladder, not a single narrow test layer.
- Unit, conformance, integration, and e2e/logging obligations are satisfied where relevant.
- Failure diagnostics are good enough that a future agent can localize the regression quickly.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.040321748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:27.810372641Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","robot","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.040321748Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:27.200351598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","title":"Add robot unit and conformance regressions for all reopened parity gaps","description":"Purpose:
Turn reopened robot mismatches into durable proof. Add module-level unit tests, fixture-backed conformance, schema validations, and focused binary tests as needed.

Logging requirement:
Failure output must clearly identify the command family, fixture, and shape/semantic dimension that regressed so debugging does not require guesswork.

Acceptance:
- Every reopened robot gap has at least one durable regression test.
- Conformance fixtures are updated or added when legacy output behavior is the source of truth.
- Test names and assertions make the user-visible contract obvious.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.572967455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:30.798970973Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity","robot","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:30.231281549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:30.798188173Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.572967455Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","title":"Add robot CLI integration and e2e scripts with detailed diagnostics and artifact capture","description":"Purpose:
Cover whole-command workflows that unit and fixture tests cannot fully validate. Add CLI integration coverage and, where useful, shell/e2e scripts that capture stdout, stderr, replay commands, and fixture context for failures.

Acceptance:
- Reopened robot workflows are exercised from the real CLI entrypoint.
- Diagnostic output is rich enough for a future agent to reproduce failures quickly.
- Scripts and tests remain deterministic and `rch`-friendly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.429726740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","parity","robot","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.428943569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3","title":"Audit and close interactive TUI workflow gaps without chasing pixel parity","description":"Background:
The TUI does not need to look exactly like the legacy Go implementation, but it does need to support the same operator workflows, navigation loops, search/discovery behaviors, and task-completion outcomes.

Goal:
Audit core TUI workflows with fresh eyes, identify interaction gaps or regressions, and improve the frankentui implementation until it covers the legacy workflow surface and is at least as usable.

Acceptance:
- Main, board, insights, graph, and history workflows are audited end-to-end.
- Missing or weaker interactions are converted into concrete implementation tasks.
- The resulting TUI is behaviorally equivalent or better for core legacy jobs, even if visual styling differs.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.981524477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.113236988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.981524477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":129,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TUI track guidance:
Judge the Rust TUI by workflow coverage, not by pixel matching. A different layout or nicer presentation is acceptable if operators can still complete the same tasks at least as effectively. Prioritize discovery, navigation, search/filter loops, detail usefulness, and mode transitions.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","title":"Audit main-view operator workflows and detail-pane usefulness against legacy jobs-to-be-done","description":"Purpose:
Audit the default TUI surface as an operator tool rather than as a rendering artifact. The question is whether a user can browse, prioritize, inspect blockers, search, sort, and decide what to do next as effectively as in legacy `bv`.

Focus:
Selection behavior, detail richness, search loops, filter transitions, sort order expectations, focus changes, and empty/no-hit states.

Outcome:
Produce a concrete list of workflow gaps and fixes, not aesthetic opinions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.345527212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:27.770328099Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["main-view","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:27.769527948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.345527212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","title":"Audit board, insights, and graph modes for workflow completeness and navigation parity","description":"Purpose:
The secondary visual modes need to support the same discovery and decision-making loops as legacy `bv`. Audit lane traversal, inline detail use, metric explanation flows, dependency navigation, search behavior, and breakpoint/layout transitions.

Done means:
We know exactly where the Rust TUI is weaker, equivalent, or already better for these modes.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.638669240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:28.350934021Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["board","graph","insights","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:28.349915327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.638669240Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.3","title":"Audit history mode and cross-mode navigation loops against real operator tasks","description":"Purpose:
History mode is easy to overclaim because individual features can exist without the whole operator workflow feeling complete. Audit commit/bead switching, file tree interaction, search modes, confidence filters, jump-back behavior, and cross-mode transitions.

Acceptance:

+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.5","title":"Strengthen TUI proof harnesses with journey coverage for audited legacy workflows","description":"Purpose:
Once workflow gaps are known, encode them into durable tests: snapshots where layout matters, keyflow/state tests where behavior matters, and e2e journeys where multi-mode operator loops matter.

Done means:
The parity story for TUI workflows can be demonstrated repeatedly, not just described.","design":"Testing ladder:
1. Unit/state tests for mode transitions and search/filter/focus behavior.
2. Snapshot coverage when layout, width breakpoints, or visible detail matter.
3. Keyflow tests for deterministic interaction loops.
4. Multi-mode journey tests with artifact capture for the highest-risk operator workflows.

The harness should prove user workflow coverage, not just keybinding existence.","acceptance_criteria":"- Audited TUI gaps are encoded into deterministic unit/state, snapshot, keyflow, and journey coverage as appropriate.
- Failure output names the affected mode, width/context, and workflow step.
- Rich diagnostics exist for non-interactive debugging.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.601446167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:28.130265865Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","testing","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.601446167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:31.607892316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","title":"Add TUI unit, state, snapshot, and keyflow regressions for audited workflow gaps","description":"Purpose:
Encode recovered TUI behaviors into deterministic tests at the right granularity: unit/state tests for transitions, snapshots for layout-sensitive states, and keyflow tests for interaction sequences.

Logging requirement:
Failures must say which mode, width/context, and operator journey step regressed so the broken interaction is obvious.

Acceptance:
- Every reopened TUI gap has durable coverage at the most appropriate layer.
- Snapshot diffs and keyflow assertions are readable enough to diagnose regressions quickly.
- Width-sensitive and focus-sensitive behaviors are covered where relevant.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:14.756563211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:35.151160620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["keyflow","parity","snapshot","testing","tui","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:33.842579451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:34.509046873Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:35.150361539Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:14.756563211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-2bpl.4","title":"Audit and close export/pages/workspace integration parity gaps","description":"Background:
Legacy `bv` is more than robot JSON and a TUI. It also includes export hooks, workspace-aware behavior, pages export and preview flows, and operator-facing deployment workflows that can quietly diverge when path semantics or bundle contracts differ.

Goal:
Rebuild confidence in the integration surfaces by auditing and then closing gaps in static export artifacts, preview/watch behavior, pages wizard flows, workspace aggregation, and hook execution semantics.

Acceptance:
- Export artifacts and runtime bundle semantics are explicitly checked against legacy expectations.
- Preview/watch/pages workflows are validated for both happy-path and failure-path behavior.
- Workspace and repo-path semantics are proven consistent across integration surfaces.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.149035060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","pages","parity","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.154964889Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":130,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Integration track guidance:
Treat path-resolution semantics as a recurring root-cause category. Recent bugs in hooks, feedback storage, preview serving, and wizard repair flow all came from seemingly minor cwd/repo/workspace assumptions. Audit those assumptions first, then fix bundle/runtime behavior.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","title":"Re-audit static export bundle semantics and viewer-runtime contracts","description":"Purpose:
Audit the exported bundle as a product artifact: asset inventory, bootstrap payloads, offline/static-host assumptions, CSP/cache behavior, SQLite or JSON sidecars, and viewer startup expectations.

Why this matters:
A pages export that exists but does not behave like the legacy artifact is not real parity.

Done means:
We have a concrete list of matching and missing bundle semantics, plus any required fix tasks.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.257337807Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bundle","export","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:32.256517849Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.937867751Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+{"id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","title":"Add shell e2e operator scripts with detailed lifecycle, watch, and artifact logging","description":"Purpose:
Add or strengthen shell-level operator journeys for export/pages/workspace workflows. Capture lifecycle logs, live-reload/watch traces, preview endpoints, wizard transcripts where appropriate, and replayable diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- The highest-risk integration workflows are exercised end to end.
- Failure logs make root-cause diagnosis fast.
- Scripts stay deterministic and suitable for CI/rch usage.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.717995396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:40.825709664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","export","logging","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:16.717995396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:40.824645031Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.5","title":"Publish a truthful parity verdict and final evidence package","description":"Background:
A parity program is only complete when the project docs, tracker, and verification evidence agree. The previous closure overclaimed readiness; this track exists to prevent that from happening again.

Goal:
Collect the evidence from the audit and implementation tracks, revise project-facing parity language to match reality, and produce a final verdict that future maintainers can trust.

Acceptance:
- README, FEATURE_PARITY, and any release-readiness claims align with measured reality.
- Residual gaps or intentional divergences are written down explicitly.
- The final verification sweep names exactly which suites and workflows were used as evidence.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.346480229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.185188324Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.346480229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","title":"Rewrite parity-facing project docs so they describe capability truthfully and precisely","description":"Purpose:
Once the audit has done its work, fix the project-facing narrative. README, FEATURE_PARITY, and architecture docs should stop implying stronger claims than we can actually support.

Acceptance:
- Claims are tied to evidence.
- Intentional divergences are explicit.
- Better-than-legacy enhancements are described without obscuring uncovered gaps.","design":"Documentation must be evidence-backed. Every strong parity claim should point to either a completed audit/fix bead, a test harness, or an explicit divergence note. Preserve all existing supported features in the narrative; do not simplify the docs by silently dropping legacy surfaces from the story.","acceptance_criteria":"- README, FEATURE_PARITY, and architecture docs state capability parity in a way that matches evidence.
- No feature or workflow is silently dropped from the parity narrative.
- Better-than-legacy improvements are described as additions, not as excuses to skip parity obligations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.736264295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.071841976Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:37.256732478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:44.630242999Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:45.454809433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:46.337960308Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:47.232085062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:48.111403844Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.4.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.071034159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:47.736264295Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","title":"Run the final parity verification sweep and publish the verdict with residual gaps","description":"Purpose:
This is the terminal verification task. It should only close when the audit and implementation tracks are done or intentionally de-scoped.

Acceptance:
- Required `rch` quality gates and targeted suites pass.
- The final verdict names what is truly equivalent, what is better, and what remains out of scope.
- The closing note is specific enough that future maintainers do not need to reverse-engineer what was checked.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:48.147962874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:41.418272160Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:48.147962874Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.5.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:41.416758901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ec","title":"TUI feature unit tests + keyflow e2e scripts","description":"WHAT: Test coverage for all TUI additions. (1) Unit/snapshot tests: mouse scroll events produce correct state transitions, help screen renders with all keybindings in correct columns, Ctrl+R triggers reload and recomputes state, priority hints overlay shows ScoreBreakdown data, export/clipboard keybindings invoke correct handlers, each new ViewMode (Actionable, Alerts, LabelDashboard, FlowMatrix, Sprint, Tree) renders without panic on empty data and on populated data, time-travel mode navigation produces correct historical state, modal system opens/closes/selects correctly. (2) Keyflow e2e scripts (scripts/test_tui_keyflows.sh) that send scripted key sequences via the test harness and verify: view mode transitions work (pressing key enters mode, Esc returns), scrolling works in all modes, search works in new modes, no panics on rapid key mashing. LOGGING: Each keyflow test logs the key sequence sent, expected state after each key, actual state. On failure, dump the full BvrApp state struct. FIXTURES: Use a 20-node test graph with cycles, labels, and parent-child relationships to exercise all views. EDGE CASES: Empty graph (0 nodes), single node, all-closed issues, graph with only cycles. ACCEPTANCE: All new TUI features have at least one happy-path and one edge-case test. Keyflow script exits 0. No panics in any scenario. PARENT: bd-3dh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:26:35.954701417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:32:04.826217105Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T21:32:04.825780617Z","close_reason":"Added 50+ new TUI tests: 27 snapshot tests (3 breakpoints x 6 newer modes + 10 interactive snapshots), 8 empty-data/edge-case no-panic tests, 7 keyflow journeys (full mode tour, sprint, modal chain, rapid switching, navigation, help, filter persistence), 5 all-modes stress tests (narrow width, single issue, all-closed, cyclic deps), 4 filter/modal integration tests. Total: 1119 tests (752 lib + 367 integration), 0 failures.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:24.420092790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:30.627211257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:31.645435903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:33.036659628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:37.100132613Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:24.955422280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:25.527845578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:25.966859669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:26.584950765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:27.410871473Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:28.171989029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:29.128372387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ec","depends_on_id":"bd-3dh.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:27:29.949349962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-2ec","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TESTING STRATEGY: TUI tests are the hardest to write well. Use the existing test harness pattern (construct BvrApp with test data, send key events, assert state). For new ViewModes, the minimum viable test is: enter the mode, verify no panic, verify some expected data appears. For complex interactions (time-travel, modals), write multi-step keyflow sequences. The keyflow e2e script is critical for catching regressions — it should be fast (<10s) and cover all view mode transitions.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:28:22Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2i6","title":"Conformance: large-dataset stress-test fixtures and adversarial edge cases","description":"Background: large/adversarial fixture coverage is a critical parity confidence lever and already partially underway.\nWork: expand and harden stress fixtures (size, shape, pathological dependencies, malformed metadata) and keep fixture intent documented.\nAcceptance: fixture suite reproduces known edge conditions and catches regression classes that minimal fixtures miss.\nTesting/logging: fixture generation and validation steps must emit detailed metadata and provenance logs.","notes":"2026-03-05: completed stress/adversarial fixture provenance hardening. Added machine-readable fixture manifest tests/testdata/fixture_metadata.json with intent, origin, provenance, expected failure signatures, categories, and record_count for stress/adversarial fixtures. Added conformance validation test stress_fixture_manifest_has_provenance_and_validated_counts in tests/conformance.rs to enforce metadata presence and JSONL count integrity. Enhanced tests/testdata/gen_stress_fixtures.py generation logs to emit provenance/intent/categories/failure-signature details from the manifest and print validation command. Updated README with explicit fixture metadata validation command. Verified via rch: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo test --test conformance stress_fixture_manifest_has_provenance_and_validated_counts -- --nocapture.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T21:53:17.916549894Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-05T04:21:51.976183578Z","closed_at":"2026-03-05T04:21:51.976118085Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2i6","depends_on_id":"bd-33w.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:32:19Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2i6","depends_on_id":"bd-33w.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T04:32:19Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
